author: CC
in_flight: DESIGN CONVERGED, CODE PARTLY WRONG-SHAPE (labeled, not faked). The durable capture of the converged architecture is `CairnCommons/intentions/I-heartbeat-callbacks-and-bus.md` — READ IT FIRST next session. Summary: TWO universal substrates — the HEARTBEAT (ground_loop = one daemon that only beats) and the BUS (the sole, owner-gated, inspectable+logged path for ALL inter-device communication, symmetric with db_domain=sole-path-to-state). Each DEVICE is its own PROCESS, asleep when idle; its SHIM (always-on, lightweight) fires its due callbacks on each beat, receives bus messages, and STARTS the device on demand. A CALLBACK is immutable/stateless ('call X when this predicate is true') — firing = kick off a separate short-lived Python process that messages the target's shim then terminates; because it's declaration it lives in class-space (device code tree). A TICKET is the OTHER species — a mutable WORKFLOW node (state machine, LEARNING->ARCHIVED), instance-space. LEARNING is a STATE; a callback is the WORKER a LEARNING node sets — don't conflate. A TRIGGER is ANYTHING THAT EVALUATES TO TRUE (open predicate — NOT the interval/date/quantity/state enum I shipped; that was a reification). A callback is evaluated WHERE ITS DATA IS OWNED (Law 6 for triggers) so only the wake-up crosses the bus, never raw data. Registration = ADVERTISE (device lists callbacks it offers) -> SUBSCRIBE ('I'll take one, value 80, here's my address') -> POKE; the device resolves its own method, the caller never knows internals. system_rackmount = THE SYSTEM DEVICE (owner of host-resource predicates: CPU/memory/disk), NOT a central scheduler. Proof is the exemplar quality-trigger (a claim evaluated true = green VALIDATION; PROVEME->PROVED is a proof-trigger) — the tester and the callback system are one substrate.
next_direction: REWORK THE CODE to the revised intentions (this is cairn task, held until Akien said 'go' on capture-first — now captured). In order: (1) strip ground_loop to the HEARTBEAT (delete run_driver / resolve / write — it only beats, finds subscribers, pulses shims); (2) BaseShim GAINS per-pulse callback-firing + bus-message receipt + on-demand device START (each device its own process); (3) callbacks = immutable declarations in the device's class-space code tree; the fire path = separate ephemeral process -> bus message -> target shim; (4) tickets = the mutable workflow species (instance-space), distinct from callbacks; (5) system_rackmount = the SYSTEM DEVICE (host-resource predicate owner); DELETE the central SchedulerService + the interval/date/quantity/state enum; (6) build the BUS as a new component — the sole comm path, db_domain-backed transit, MCP adapter at the edge (swappable), per-device channels (announce/personal/info/debug), envelopes carrying why+causality. Re-prove each under the tester. INTENTIONS ALREADY REVISED this session (design ahead of code, Law 3-labeled): cairn/ground_loop/intention+why.json (heartbeat-only), cairn/base/intention+why.json (shim one-loop resolved = callback-firing). system_rackmount + ground_loop intentions still say 'ticket' in places -> update to 'callback' during rework. THEN the spine resumes (MAP.md:892): inference domain -> web server. The chassis 'rack' (bus + shims) is now the bus work above, not a separate device.
open_threads: THE ARCHITECTURE IS IN CairnCommons/intentions/I-heartbeat-callbacks-and-bus.md — the single source; this slate summarizes it. It sharpens/partly supersedes MAP.md's operational-driver + host/rack + peers-fire-in-two-modes sketches and tickets/state-machine-physics.json (which was updated this session to point at the new model).

CODE STANDS AT THE WRONG SHAPE, honestly labeled: ground_loop (584aa74) is a run_driver EXECUTOR and system_rackmount (5cb593a) is a central SCHEDULER with an interval/date/quantity/state enum — both the goof the design corrects. Their proofs are green for the WRONG shape. The revised intentions say so (Law 3). Do NOT treat these as done; rework per next_direction. sudo_relay (d4400c7/28dec58) is CORRECT and stands — privilege is a system service in the same family as the future system-device callbacks.

THREE REIFY-VS-FLOW CATCHES this session (all caught by Akien at n=1): (1) rack=scheduler [the rack is the chassis: bus+shims]; (2) ground_loop=executor [it's ONLY the heartbeat; firing is in the shim; lost 'a ticket is the same unit no matter what fires it']; (3) triggers=a 4-kind enum [a trigger is anything that evaluates to true]. Tell: I freeze examples/metaphors into taxonomies. Memory [[cc-reify-vs-flow]] updated with these. This is why the design conversation was worth more than the code — the code was fast and wrong; the model is slow and right.

BUS DESIGN (detail in the intention doc): sole comm path (no direct inter-device calls, ever); one substrate, surfaces are VIEWS (kills UU's bus-vs-webserver split); durable transit via db_domain (owner-gated, logged+inspectable free); record-of-truth vs diagnostic-surface as physics (Law 7 — permanent vs collapsible/expiring); per-device channels announce/personal/info/debug (Murderbot feeds, Wells); every envelope carries why+causality -> replayable causal record, a woken device rebuilds context from its feed; human is a NATIVE participant (channels), web server is a view; protocol at the edge (MCP = today's lingua franca adapter, swappable — 'doesn't even have to be MCP'). Backpressure = a data-accumulated trigger (a filling channel pokes its reader) — same primitive as scheduling.

system_rackmount A/B QUESTION SETTLED -> A: it survives as THE SYSTEM DEVICE, owner of host-resource predicates (CPU/memory/disk), serving threshold callbacks on request (advertise/subscribe/poke). 'Abstracts host services device-independently' cashes out to exactly this. NOT a central scheduler.

CONVENTION (carried, unchanged): commit trailer is plain `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>` (no Claude-Session line, no 1M-context variant). GitHub hosting-only — never add CI/workflows. Proofs run bare (python3, sys.path parents[N]). Commit often, push at 'smells like done'. Minimalism [[build-minimal-grow-against-need]]; force-the-why-in-structure; intent+impl share an address (Law 5).

STANDING (unchanged): the emit-chokepoint that ENFORCES the state-machine table (Law 4) still unbuilt; node-state vocab (PROVED/LEARNING) not yet wired into device.state(); the three CLAUDE.md physics IOUs (db_domain / inference_domain / port-5432) retire fully only when the tester IMPORT-SCAN tooth makes 'sole path' physics. sudo_relay capability: CC self-serves root via the Cairn-native daemon when Akien runs it ([[sudo-relay-self-serve-ops]]).

MEMORY refreshed this session (~/.claude, not in git): next-direction-session-continuity.md (latest = this design reshape), [[cc-reify-vs-flow]] (+3 instances), [[rack-vs-system-rackmount-vs-scheduler]] (refined to the heartbeat/callback/bus model). MEMORY.md index current.
